subject: Mathematics
title: Fun at the Fair

sections.heading: Fun at the Fair
sections.content:
Rupal loves going to the fair. She visits the local fair with
her friends. Her mother gave her `50. She spent most of it
on her favourite rides.
Talk about the things that children enjoy in fairs, whom do they
go with, what do they buy and how much money do they spend
there.
10 Fun at the Fair
Reprint 2026-27
A friend of Rupal has `8 left out of `50. She wanted to know
how much money she spent. Let us see her way of finding it.
`8 + `20 = `28
`28 + `20 = `48
`48 + `2 = `50
`42

I have spent `42.
If you had `3 left when your father gave you `40 for the fair,
how many rupees did you spend? Find out.

Ask the children to observe other currency notes and discuss their features.
Discuss with them the specifications, such as the lines on the
border of the currency notes, and their number on each note for
specially abled people.

Pop the Balloon
Matru comes across a ‘Pop the balloon’ game. He will get 3
chances for `10. Let us see how much money Matru wins.

following balloons?

Let us Do
Create more such contexts and ask questions whether children
will add or subtract. Inculcate the value of saving money in the
children through a discussion.
Project Work
Whenever you go to the market or shop with your parents, make
a list of the things that you buy and the total money you spend.
Have you seen
that sometimes we
pay using mobile
phones?
A. Jayant spent `25 on pens and `40 on
notebooks. How much money did he
spend in total?
B. Meera has `43 with her. Kanika has
`14 more than Meera. How much
money does Kanika have?
C. A packet of bread costs `30 and a
chocolate costs `60. How much money
does Vivek need to buy these items?
D. Ajay has `58 with him. He spent `48 in the market. How
much money is left with him now?

learningObjectives: Understand the key concepts of Fun at the Fair
Identify and explain the main ideas covered in this chapter
Apply knowledge from this chapter to real-world situations
Recall important terms and definitions from Fun at the Fair
